channel.title: Updates on Travis Beckham
channel.description: Recent content in Updates on Travis Beckham

item.description: Today, Tim showed us the real future of the iphone... "Sorry, you must look at the ad to resume playback." - Siri

On Thursdays, I'll try to dig up some old work that I find interesting. I'll start with a little racing game I designed and coded in 2005. While at BR, I had the oppurtunity to build many fun games for McDonald's. I really loved these projects because they allowed me to do some illustration, design a little, develop the gameplay, and write some rather intense ActionScript.
I loved the first 2 seasons of Narcos, and was worried that the 3rd season would fall apart since the Escobar storyline was over. I didn't need to worry at all, it's the same fantastic show.
Getting the day started with "+/-" by Buke and Gase.
The War on Drugs has a new album out, and I'm loving it. They are going to be in Chicago on my birthday... mmm, maybe I should grab some tickets. Listen on Spotfiy.

If you're just learning Hugo like me, you may want to start with Go's template documentation. The conditional sytax tripped me up for a minute. If you're accustomed to writing conditionals like this: if ($x == $y) if ($x && $y) if ($x == $y || $x == $z) This is how it would look in a Go template: {{ if eq $x $y }} {{ if and $x $y }} {{ if or (eq $x $y) (eq $x $z) }} In a Go template, eq is a function that you pass arguments to, not a comparison operator like you would use in JavaScript.
Welp, this site has just been switched over to Hugo! There's still some template cleanup I want to do, but overall it was fairly simple to migrate from Jekyll.
I gave Hugo a test run today, and I'm loving it so far. It seems very powerful, and so easy to setup. I may be switching from Jekyll soon.
